chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: The Canada is sorry to hear you only saw a red ball and not a yellow usual one

chat.author: Robert Moskowitz
chat.text: Of course it our CO2 that we have been exporting north to melt the permafrost...

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: Rather like HHITs but tied primarily to DNS rather than primarily to an IPv6 address, and extended to capabilities?

chat.author: Heather Flanagan
chat.text: Isn’t the answer to “how to bring protocol $a and protocol $b together” always “via another layer of abstraction”?

chat.author: Robert Moskowitz
chat.text: Until there are too many layers

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: The practical cyber security side meeting is Weds 4pm, Grand Klimt 3

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: Confidential Computing and Digital Sovereignty: try DIN as a research group to work on this?

chat.author: Muhammad Usama Sardar
chat.text:
Lixia Zhang said:


Confidential Computing and Digital Sovereignty: try DIN as a research group to work on this?


Sure, thanks a lot Lixia. I'll check potential overlaps. Happy to do this as part of this RG if that fits the scope.

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: DINRG is not meeting this time (unfortunately). We can start the discussion on the mailing list after Vienna. Meanwhile, you might be interested in taking a look at the last two talks at the DINRG IETF125 meeting.

chat.author: Muhammad Usama Sardar
chat.text: oh that's a pity that DINRG is not meeting. Will join the mailing list, and happy to have a chat with you and Dirk to move this work forward.
